> **Estimated time:** 4-6 h ┬À **Difficulty:** ­şşí mixed API ┬À **Labels:** `good-first-issue` `help wanted` `provider`
>
> **Maintainer pair-review available** ÔÇö comment below before you start; you will not be left alone with the first PR.

## What we want

A working **DirectAdmin adapter skeleton** living at `providers/directadmin/` that implements the [`providers.HostingProvider`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/providers/provider.go) interface against the [**DirectAdmin Admin / User API**](https://docs.directadmin.com/developer/api/legacy-api/index.html). After this issue is closed:

- `go build ./...` continues to succeed.
- `webox doctor` reports DirectAdmin as a *registered* provider.
- One read-only method (`CheckStatus`) actually calls the DA API end-to-end with a sanitised fixture.
- The other interface methods return `directadmin.ErrNotImplemented`.

Real production-grade implementations land in **Sprint 19** (see [`docs/ROADMAP.md`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md)). This issue gets the package on disk and proves the auth + parser contracts.

## Why DirectAdmin matters

DirectAdmin runs a meaningful slice of European shared hosting (Hostinger, Hosteurope, Netcup, RoseHosting). It is a sweet-spot panel for the "less than cPanel but more than CyberPanel" segment. The API mixes **legacy form-encoded responses** with newer **JSON responses** ÔÇö the parser layer is the interesting work here, and getting it right unlocks ~5 M websites.

## How to start (30 seconds)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dilitS/webox.git
cd webox
make build
./bin/webox provider new directadmin --preset directadmin
```

That command scaffolds `providers/directadmin/{provider,parsers,doc,fixture}.go` with stubs pre-configured for the DA dual-format response shape.

Then follow [`docs/contributing/PROVIDER.md`](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/docs/contributing/PROVIDER.md). Sections especially relevant:

- ┬ğ3 *Fixture sourcing & sanitisation* ÔÇö DA `CMD_API_SHOW_USER_CONFIG` is a good first capture because it has no destructive side effects and exercises both response formats.
- ┬ğ4 *TDD parsers* ÔÇö table-driven cases for the form-encoded vs JSON branches.
- ┬ğ6 *Auth flow* ÔÇö DA uses Login Keys (recommended) or basic auth (legacy). Use the keyring path from [`docs/SECURITY.md` ┬ğ3](https://github.com/dilitS/webox/blob/main/docs/SECURITY.md).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `providers/directadmin/` exists, generated by `webox provider new directadmin --preset directadmin`.
- [ ] `go build ./...` passes.
- [ ] `directadmin.Provider.CheckStatus(ctx)` actually calls `CMD_API_SHOW_USER_CONFIG` (or any read-only endpoint of equivalent cost) and parses both the form-encoded *and* JSON response shape.
- [ ] At least one fixture per response shape captured from a real DA sandbox and sanitised.
- [ ] All other interface methods return `directadmin.ErrNotImplemented` wrapped with the method name.
- [ ] Coverage ÔëÑ 70 % for the new package.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` green.
- [ ] CHANGELOG `[Unreleased] / Added` entry.
- [ ] `docs/providers/directadmin.md` `TO BE VERIFIED` markers resolved for ┬ğ1 *Capability probe* and ┬ğ2 *Auth flow*.

## What we will NOT accept

- Basic-auth credentials in fixtures (use Login Keys, sanitise them).
- A single parser that "tries JSON first, falls back to form-encoded" ÔÇö the response format must be picked deterministically (Content-Type header check, or per-endpoint table).
- `Update()` / `View()` modifications.
- Hardcoded `directadmin` strings in business logic outside `providers/directadmin/`.
